name: Anthonys Limousine Svc
districts_or_customers: Special needs and senior individuals in metro Detroit; non-emergency wheelchair-accessible transport; no confirmed school district contracts
recent_news: Per BBB, business started 10/1/1997; founder Anthony has passed and family (wife Carol and son) continue operations; expanded into special-needs/senior niche (per company About page, undated).
fit_notes: Very small (~10 employees, ~$743K rev) livery operator with some special-needs/wheelchair work; not a school-bus contractor. Likely too small and outside core school-bus profile to be a meaningful tuck-in for Summit; succession theme exists (founder deceased) but scale is sub-threshold.

name: Holiday Limousine
districts_or_customers: No confirmed school district or special-needs contracts identified in public sources
recent_news: No public M&A, growth, or safety news identified; no working company website found in search.
fit_notes: Tiny (~11 employees, ~$722K rev) Atlantic City limousine operator; no confirmation it provides school-bus or special-needs student transport. Sub-scale and off-thesis for Summit; pass unless additional evidence emerges of contracted SPED routes.

name: Orangeburg Limo
districts_or_customers: No confirmed school district contracts; Batesburg-Leesville is served by Lexington County School District Three but no link found.
recent_news: No public M&A, ownership, growth, or safety news identified; no company website found.
fit_notes: Small (~15 employees, ~$718K rev) SC livery operator with no confirmed school-bus operations. SC public-school transport is largely state-run, limiting contract upside. Off-thesis for Summit tuck-in.
